title: Remove “Create a Workform” ad from form’s Thank You page
excerpt:
The “Thank you” page (or work form response page) has currently a message for WorkForms with a button to create a new form. 
Could you please implement a way to have the optional creation of forms after form response? At…

posts.author: pedro.duarte (Pedro Duarte)
posts.cookedHtml:
The “Thank you” page (or work form response page) has currently a message for WorkForms with a button to create a new form.

Could you please implement a way to have the optional creation of forms after form response? At least for paying customers like us and eventually in the same way you made monday.com branding optional.

Thank you,
pedro.

posts.author: pedro.duarte (Pedro Duarte)
posts.cookedHtml:
Update: I believe that the “Redirect URL” will serve to redirect the response to another webpage, where everything can be defined is a custom way.

The challenge is that this will only work for people who have, or administer, a website.
Those who pay and just wish to make forms will not have this possibility.

Warning: this “Redirect URL” is untested by me but taking into account my programming experience I believe it should work as explained above.

posts.author: Benjamin (Benjamin)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can remove the ad.
Navigate to the WorkForms Submission View settings. There you can choose to hide the branding for the Form. This, strangely, removes the ad on the thank you screen.
Check this articel for more information: WorkForms settings and permissions – Support (monday.com)

posts.author: ND-Dan (Dan)
posts.cookedHtml: I am using the Redirect URL with my form. It works, but the advertisement still shows for 10-15 seconds before the redirect happens.

posts.author: pedro.duarte (Pedro Duarte)
posts.cookedHtml:
In the meantime, monday.com supports this. No more advertisement: great from monday.com to listen to the user base.
I haven’t tested it with URL though.

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah that drove us crazy too. We send forms to external clients and having someone elses branding on the thank you page looked unprofessional.
We moved to FormFlow and theres zero forced branding or ads. You control the logo, the theme, the thank you page, everything. Looks like its our own system now which is exactly what we needed.
